ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: By the star when it descends,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: By the stars when they fade away!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Your companion [Muhammad] has not strayed, nor has he erred,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Your fellow man is neither misguided nor astray.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Nor does he speak from [his own] inclination.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Nor does he speak of his own whims.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: It is not but a revelation revealed,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: It is only a revelation sent down ˹to him˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Taught to him by one intense in strength -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He has been taught by one ˹angel˺ of mighty power

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: One of soundness. And he rose to [his] true form
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and great perfection, who once rose to ˹his˺ true form

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: While he was in the higher [part of the] horizon.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: while on the highest point above the horizon,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then he approached and descended
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: then he approached ˹the Prophet˺, coming so close

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And was at a distance of two bow lengths or nearer.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: that he was only two arms-lengths away or even less.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And he revealed to His Servant what he revealed.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then Allah revealed to His servant what He revealed ˹through Gabriel˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The heart did not lie [about] what it saw.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: The ˹Prophet’s˺ heart did not doubt what he saw.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So will you dispute with him over what he saw?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: How can you ˹O pagans˺ then dispute with him regarding what he saw?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And he certainly saw him in another descent
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And he certainly saw that ˹angel descend˺ a second time

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: At the Lote Tree of the Utmost Boundary -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: at the Lote Tree of the most extreme limit ˹in the seventh heaven˺—

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Near it is the Garden of Refuge -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: near which is the Garden of ˹Eternal˺ Residence—

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: When there covered the Lote Tree that which covered [it].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: while the Lote Tree was overwhelmed with ˹heavenly˺ splendours!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The sight [of the Prophet] did not swerve, nor did it transgress [its limit].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: The ˹Prophet’s˺ sight never wandered, nor did it overreach.

ayahs.textArabic: لَقَدۡ رَأَىٰ مِنۡ ءَايَٰتِ رَبِّهِ ٱلۡكُبۡرَىٰٓ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Laqad raa min ayati rabbihi alkubra
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He certainly saw of the greatest signs of his Lord.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He certainly saw some of his Lord’s greatest signs.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So have you considered al-Lat and al-'Uzza?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Now, have you considered ˹the idols of˺ Lât and ’Uzza,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And Manat, the third - the other one?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and the third one, Manât, as well?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Is the male for you and for Him the female?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Do you ˹prefer to˺ have sons while ˹you attribute˺ to Him daughters?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: That, then, is an unjust division.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then this is ˹truly˺ a biased distribution!

ayahs.textArabic: إِنۡ هِيَ إِلَّآ أَسۡمَآءٞ سَمَّيۡتُمُوهَآ أَنتُمۡ وَءَابَآؤُكُم مَّآ أَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ بِهَا مِن سُلۡطَٰنٍۚ إِن يَتَّبِعُونَ إِلَّا ٱلظَّنَّ وَمَا تَهۡوَى ٱلۡأَنفُسُۖ وَلَقَدۡ جَآءَهُم مِّن رَّبِّهِمُ ٱلۡهُدَىٰٓ
ayahs.textTransliteration: In hiya illa asmaon sammaytumooha antum waabaokum ma anzala Allahu biha min sultanin in yattabiAAoona illa alththanna wama tahwa alanfusu walaqad jaahum min rabbihimu alhuda
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They are not but [mere] names you have named them - you and your forefathers - for which Allah has sent down no authority. They follow not except assumption and what [their] souls desire, and there has already come to them from their Lord guidance.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: These ˹idols˺ are mere names that you and your forefathers have made up—a practice Allah has never authorized. They follow nothing but ˹inherited˺ assumptions and whatever ˹their˺ souls desire, although ˹true˺ guidance has already come to them from their Lord.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or is there for man whatever he wishes?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Or should every person ˹simply˺ have whatever ˹intercessors˺ they desire?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Rather, to Allah belongs the Hereafter and the first [life].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: In fact, to Allah ˹alone˺ belongs this world and the next.

ayahs.textArabic: ۞ وَكَم مِّن مَّلَكٖ فِي ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ لَا تُغۡنِي شَفَٰعَتُهُمۡ شَيۡـًٔا إِلَّا مِنۢ بَعۡدِ أَن يَأۡذَنَ ٱللَّهُ لِمَن يَشَآءُ وَيَرۡضَىٰٓ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wakam min malakin fee alssamawati la tughnee shafaAAatuhum shayan illa min baAAdi an yathana Allahu liman yashao wayarda
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And how many angels there are in the heavens whose intercession will not avail at all except [only] after Allah has permitted [it] to whom He wills and approves.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Imagine˺ how many ˹noble˺ angels are in the heavens! ˹Even˺ their intercession would be of no benefit whatsoever, until Allah gives permission to whoever He wills and ˹only for the people He˺ approves.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤۡمِنُونَ بِٱلۡأٓخِرَةِ لَيُسَمُّونَ ٱلۡمَلَٰٓئِكَةَ تَسۡمِيَةَ ٱلۡأُنثَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna allatheena la yuminoona bialakhirati layusammoona almalaikata tasmiyata alontha
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, those who do not believe in the Hereafter name the angels female names,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, those who do not believe in the Hereafter label angels as female,

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَا لَهُم بِهِۦ مِنۡ عِلۡمٍۖ إِن يَتَّبِعُونَ إِلَّا ٱلظَّنَّۖ وَإِنَّ ٱلظَّنَّ لَا يُغۡنِي مِنَ ٱلۡحَقِّ شَيۡـٔٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wama lahum bihi min AAilmin in yattabiAAoona illa alththanna wainna alththanna la yughnee mina alhaqqi shayan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they have thereof no knowledge. They follow not except assumption, and indeed, assumption avails not against the truth at all.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: although they have no knowledge ˹in support˺ of this. They follow nothing but ˹inherited˺ assumptions. And surely assumptions can in no way replace the truth.

ayahs.textArabic: فَأَعۡرِضۡ عَن مَّن تَوَلَّىٰ عَن ذِكۡرِنَا وَلَمۡ يُرِدۡ إِلَّا ٱلۡحَيَوٰةَ ٱلدُّنۡيَا
ayahs.textTransliteration: FaaAArid AAan man tawalla AAan thikrina walam yurid illa alhayata alddunya
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So turn away from whoever turns his back on Our message and desires not except the worldly life.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So turn away ˹O Prophet˺ from whoever has shunned Our Reminder, only seeking the ˹fleeting˺ life of this world.

ayahs.textArabic: ذَٰلِكَ مَبۡلَغُهُم مِّنَ ٱلۡعِلۡمِۚ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ أَعۡلَمُ بِمَن ضَلَّ عَن سَبِيلِهِۦ وَهُوَ أَعۡلَمُ بِمَنِ ٱهۡتَدَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Thalika mablaghuhum mina alAAilmi inna rabbaka huwa aAAlamu biman dalla AAan sabeelihi wahuwa aAAlamu bimani ihtada
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: That is their sum of knowledge. Indeed, your Lord is most knowing of who strays from His way, and He is most knowing of who is guided.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: This is the extent of their knowledge. Surely your Lord knows best who has strayed from His Way and who is ˹rightly˺ guided.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلِلَّهِ مَا فِي ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِي ٱلۡأَرۡضِ لِيَجۡزِيَ ٱلَّذِينَ أَسَٰٓـُٔواْ بِمَا عَمِلُواْ وَيَجۡزِيَ ٱلَّذِينَ أَحۡسَنُواْ بِٱلۡحُسۡنَى
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walillahi ma fee alssamawati wama fee alardi liyajziya allatheena asaoo bima AAamiloo wayajziya allatheena ahsanoo bialhusna
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And to Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth - that He may recompense those who do evil with [the penalty of] what they have done and recompense those who do good with the best [reward] -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: To Allah ˹alone˺ belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth so that He may reward the evildoers according to what they did, and reward the good-doers with the finest reward—

ayahs.textArabic: ٱلَّذِينَ يَجۡتَنِبُونَ كَبَٰٓئِرَ ٱلۡإِثۡمِ وَٱلۡفَوَٰحِشَ إِلَّا ٱللَّمَمَۚ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ وَٰسِعُ ٱلۡمَغۡفِرَةِۚ هُوَ أَعۡلَمُ بِكُمۡ إِذۡ أَنشَأَكُم مِّنَ ٱلۡأَرۡضِ وَإِذۡ أَنتُمۡ أَجِنَّةٞ فِي بُطُونِ أُمَّهَٰتِكُمۡۖ فَلَا تُزَكُّوٓاْ أَنفُسَكُمۡۖ هُوَ أَعۡلَمُ بِمَنِ ٱتَّقَىٰٓ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Allatheena yajtaniboona kabaira alithmi waalfawahisha illa allamama inna rabbaka wasiAAu almaghfirati huwa aAAlamu bikum ith anshaakum mina alardi waith antum ajinnatun fee butooni ommahatikum fala tuzakkoo anfusakum huwa aAAlamu bimani ittaqa
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Those who avoid the major sins and immoralities, only [committing] slight ones. Indeed, your Lord is vast in forgiveness. He was most knowing of you when He produced you from the earth and when you were fetuses in the wombs of your mothers. So do not claim yourselves to be pure; He is most knowing of who fears Him.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: those who avoid major sins and shameful deeds, despite ˹stumbling on˺ minor sins. Surely your Lord is infinite in forgiveness. He knew well what would become of you as He created you from the earth and while you were ˹still˺ fetuses in the wombs of your mothers. So do not ˹falsely˺ elevate yourselves. He knows best who is ˹truly˺ righteous.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Have you seen the one who turned away
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Have you seen the one who turned away ˹from Islam,˺

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And gave a little and [then] refrained?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and ˹initially˺ paid a little ˹for his salvation˺, and then stopped?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Does he have knowledge of the unseen, so he sees?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Does he have the knowledge of the unseen so that he sees ˹the Hereafter˺?

ayahs.textArabic: أَمۡ لَمۡ يُنَبَّأۡ بِمَا فِي صُحُفِ مُوسَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Am lam yunabba bima fee suhufi moosa
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or has he not been informed of what was in the scriptures of Moses
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Or has he not been informed of what is in the Scripture of Moses,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [of] Abraham, who fulfilled [his obligations] -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and ˹that of˺ Abraham, who ˹perfectly˺ fulfilled ˹his covenant˺?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: That no bearer of burdens will bear the burden of another
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹They state˺ that no soul burdened with sin will bear the burden of another,

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَن لَّيۡسَ لِلۡإِنسَٰنِ إِلَّا مَا سَعَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waan laysa lilinsani illa ma saAAa
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And that there is not for man except that [good] for which he strives
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and that each person will only have what they endeavoured towards,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And that his effort is going to be seen -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and that ˹the outcome of˺ their endeavours will be seen ˹in their record˺,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then he will be recompensed for it with the fullest recompense
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: then they will be fully rewarded,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And that to your Lord is the finality
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and that to your Lord ˹alone˺ is the ultimate return ˹of all things˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And that it is He who makes [one] laugh and weep
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Moreover, He is the One Who brings about joy and sadness.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And that it is He who causes death and gives life
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And He is the One Who gives life and causes death.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And that He creates the two mates - the male and female -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And He created the pairs—males and females—

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: From a sperm-drop when it is emitted
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: from a sperm-drop when it is emitted.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And that [incumbent] upon Him is the next creation
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And it is upon Him to bring about re-creation.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And that it is He who enriches and suffices
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And He is the One Who enriches and impoverishes.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And that it is He who is the Lord of Sirius
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And He alone is the Lord of Sirius.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And that He destroyed the first [people of] 'Aad
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And He destroyed the first ˹people of˺ ’Ȃd,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And Thamud - and He did not spare [them] -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and ˹then˺ Thamûd, sparing no one.

ayahs.textArabic: وَقَوۡمَ نُوحٖ مِّن قَبۡلُۖ إِنَّهُمۡ كَانُواْ هُمۡ أَظۡلَمَ وَأَطۡغَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waqawma noohin min qablu innahum kanoo hum athlama waatgha
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And the people of Noah before. Indeed, it was they who were [even] more unjust and oppressing.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And before ˹that He destroyed˺ the people of Noah, who were truly far worse in wrongdoing and transgression.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And the overturned towns He hurled down
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And ˹it was˺ He ˹Who˺ turned the cities ˹of Sodom and Gomorrah˺ upside down.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And covered them by that which He covered.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: How overwhelming was what covered ˹them˺!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then which of the favors of your Lord do you doubt?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Now, which of your Lord’s favours will you dispute?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: This [Prophet] is a warner like the former warners.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: This ˹Prophet˺ is a warner like earlier ones.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The Approaching Day has approached.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: The approaching ˹Hour˺ has drawn near.

ayahs.textArabic: لَيۡسَ لَهَا مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ كَاشِفَةٌ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Laysa laha min dooni Allahi kashifatun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Of it, [from those] besides Allah, there is no remover.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: None but Allah can disclose it.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then at this statement do you wonder?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Do you find this revelation astonishing,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And you laugh and do not weep
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: laughing ˹at it˺ and not weeping ˹in awe˺,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: While you are proudly sporting?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: while persisting in heedlessness?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So prostrate to Allah and worship [Him].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Instead, prostrate to Allah and worship ˹Him alone˺!
